revisedPrompt:
Create exactly one PNG image: a 1024x512 pixel-art sprite sheet for a game-development asset. Use the provided reference image as the exact character identity reference: match Mito's proportions, head-to-body ratio, body width, limb thickness, palette, outline style, pixel density, and overall scale. This is re-posing the referenced character, not redesigning him.

SHEET CONTRACT:
- Exact canvas size: 1024x512 px.
- 2 rows x 4 columns, 8 frames total.
- Each frame cell is 256x256 px.
- TOP row = frames 1,2,3,4 left to right. BOTTOM row = frames 5,6,7,8 left to right.
- Do not draw grid lines, separator lines, cell borders, frame numbers, labels, text, UI, logos, or watermarks.
- Solid #FF00FF chroma-key background filling every empty pixel, no gradients.
- One character per cell, same character identity and exact same scale in every cell.
- Keep clear solid magenta margin between figure/sword and all cell edges.
- No detached fragments, no isolated pixel islands, no seam slivers, no dust specks, no leftover effects.

STYLE:
- Crisp high-resolution 16-bit-plus SNES-style pixel art with readable pixel clusters.
- No antialiasing blur.
- Consistent bright cheerful palette.
- Dark one-pixel outline where useful.
- Top-left light source.
- Top-down 3/4 action-RPG view, facing DOWN toward the viewer in EVERY frame: face and chest square to viewer, never sideways.
- Feet on the same ground line in every cell.
- Character body about 70% of cell height so extended sword always fits inside the cell.

CHARACTER IDENTITY:
Mito, an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer. Ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, calm amber eyes. Bright mythical-Japan traveler outfit, NOT samurai, NOT ninja: white tube-sleeved top, short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, vermilion braided cord belt-sash with small tassel, necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At left hip: sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers, festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana.

ANIMATION: ONE-DIRECTION IAI / BATTOU-JUTSU draw-cut, 8-frame continuous motion, no motion trail, no swoosh, no afterimage, no energy effect. Draw ONLY the solid body, scabbard, hilt, sword, sacred rope, and tiny paper streamers.

Frame 1: IAI STANCE. Knees slightly bent, body lowered and coiled, left hand gripping the white scabbard at left hip, right hand resting on hilt, blade still fully sheathed, eyes focused.
Frame 2: Deeper crouch, maximum tension, still sheathed, charge-loop pose. Same facing down, same scale.
Frame 3: DRAW BEGINS. Blade halfway out of scabbard, body beginning to twist while chest and face remain viewer-facing, scabbard held at left hip.
Frame 4: BATTOU key pose. Blade fully drawn in one lightning rising cut in front of him toward the viewer, arms extended forward-downward, blade arcing up-forward, decisive pose, no effects.
Frame 5: FOLLOW-THROUGH. Swing decelerates past his body, paper streamers on hilt trailing as solid drawn pieces, no detached residue.
Frame 6: Settling. Weight forward, blade low, calm control, feet still on same ground line.
Frame 7: NOTO. He guides the blade back toward the scabbard mouth at the left hip.
Frame 8: Sheathed again, calm stance, loops cleanly back to frame 1.

STRICT AVOID:
No Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos, or likeness. No samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature, no letter emblems, no text, no watermark. No body part, sword piece, or stray fragment may cross a cell boundary or float detached anywhere.
